methods.name: EnableAnalysisMeshes
methods.signature: public void EnableAnalysisMeshes(ObjectType objectType, bool enable)
methods.summary: Activates saving of analysis meshes for specific types of objects. If you do not specify the state for an object type, its default is used. Currently SubD mesh saving is disabled by default, while Brep and Extrusion is enabled.

params.name: objectType
params.doc: The object type. Mostly mesh, brep, extrusion and SubD (or their flag combinations) make sense here. DO NOT specify a 'filter' or sub-object type.

params.name: enable
params.doc: If false, disables saving for this object type.

methods.name: EnableRenderMeshes
methods.signature: public void EnableRenderMeshes(ObjectType objectType, bool enable)
methods.summary: Activates saving of render meshes for specific types of objects. If you do not specify the state for an object type, its default is used. Specifically, currently SubD mesh saving is disabled by default, while Brep and Extrusion is on.

params.name: objectType
params.doc: The object type. Mostly brep, extrusion and SubD (or their flag combinations) make sense here. DO NOT specify a 'filter' or sub-object type.

params.name: enable
params.doc: If false, disables saving for this object type.

properties.name: SaveAnalysisMeshes
properties.summary: Include analysis meshes in the file. Default is true

properties.name: SaveRenderMeshes
properties.summary: Include Render meshes in the file. Default is true

properties.name: SaveUserData
properties.summary: Include custom user data in the file. Default is true

properties.name: Version
properties.summary: File version. Default is major version number of this assembly version.Must be in range [2; current version].Alternatively, 0 is a placeholder for the last valid version.Rhino can read its current version, plus earlier file versions except 1.Use latest version when possible.
